tool_name: AI Use Case Intake and Triage Worksheet
intended_use: Front-end intake and triage tool for proposed public health AI use cases before scoring, vendor discussion, procurement, pilot planning, or implementation.
short_description: Captures the proposed AI use case, public health purpose, workflow, data, users, affected populations, risks, readiness, equity considerations, and recommended governance review pathway.
completion_guidance: Complete Part 1 before any AI tool is purchased, piloted, configured, or used with public health data or public-facing work.
Use Part 2 to describe the proposed use case in plain language, including the public health problem, affected workflow, intended users, and expected benefit.
Use Parts 3 through 7 to screen data, privacy, security, equity, workflow, vendor, legal, and operational considerations.
Use Part 8 to assign an initial triage level and Part 9 to document the governance decision, next steps, and required evidence.

playbook_integration.primary_play_title: Prioritize use cases through governance, equity, and feasibility review

sections.title: Intake Summary
sections.description: Basic identifying information and early screening details for the proposed AI use case.

fields.label: Is this an internal productivity use?
Is this a consequential public health use?
fields.help_text: Consequential uses may affect services, surveillance, resource allocation, public messaging, eligibility, enforcement, partner obligations, or community outcomes.

sections.title: Public Health Purpose and Use Case Description

fields.label: Public health problem or opportunity
fields.help_text: Describe the problem the AI-supported workflow is intended to address. Include who is affected, why the problem matters, and what is not working well today.

fields.label: Proposed AI-supported activity
fields.help_text: Describe what the AI system, model, or tool would do. Avoid vendor language. Explain the actual task in plain language.

fields.label: Intended users
fields.help_text: List the staff, partners, contractors, or community-facing users who would interact with the AI-supported workflow.

fields.label: Affected population or communities
fields.help_text: Identify populations, jurisdictions, facilities, programs, or communities that may be affected by the AI-supported use case.

fields.label: Expected benefit
fields.help_text: Describe the expected public health value, such as faster response, improved data quality, better targeting of services, improved communication, workload reduction, or stronger situational awareness.

fields.label: Success measures
fields.help_text: List 2 to 5 measurable indicators that would show whether the use case is successful.

fields.label: Alternative approaches
fields.help_text: What non-AI or lower-risk approaches have been considered? Why might AI be appropriate here?

fields.label: Data sources
fields.help_text: List all data sources, systems, documents, databases, APIs, files, or public information that would be used.

fields.label: Data quality concerns
fields.help_text: Describe completeness, timeliness, standardization, bias, missingness, duplicate records, terminology, or linkage issues.

fields.label: Data sharing or access changes
fields.help_text: Will new people, systems, vendors, or partners gain access to data? Describe what changes.

fields.label: System integration needs
fields.help_text: Describe whether the use case requires APIs, file transfers, EHR/HIE integration, FHIR, HL7, dashboards, case management systems, cloud services, or other infrastructure.

fields.label: Data retention and records
fields.help_text: Describe what input data, outputs, prompts, logs, model results, and decisions would be retained, for how long, and where.

fields.label: Current workflow
fields.help_text: Briefly describe the current workflow, including key steps, handoffs, decisions, and pain points.

fields.label: Proposed workflow change
fields.help_text: Describe what would change if the AI-supported workflow is implemented.

fields.label: Human review point
fields.help_text: Where will a person review, approve, reject, revise, or override the AI output?

fields.label: Decision authority
fields.help_text: Who remains accountable for decisions, communications, approvals, or actions supported by the AI tool?

fields.label: User training needs
fields.help_text: What staff need training before using, reviewing, or supervising the AI-supported workflow?

fields.label: Failure response
fields.help_text: What should staff do if the AI output appears wrong, biased, unsafe, incomplete, or inappropriate?

sections.title: Equity, Civil Rights, Accessibility, and Community Impact

fields.label: Potential equity risks
fields.help_text: Could the use case affect groups differently by race, ethnicity, language, disability, geography, age, income, insurance status, housing status, immigration status, or other factors?

fields.label: Missing voices
fields.help_text: Which communities, staff groups, partners, or users should be consulted before moving forward?

fields.label: Language access and accessibility
fields.help_text: Will outputs or communications need plain language, translation, disability accessibility, or culturally appropriate review?

fields.label: Community trust considerations
fields.help_text: Could this use case raise concerns about surveillance, targeting, stigma, eligibility, enforcement, transparency, or public trust?

fields.label: Mitigation steps
fields.help_text: What safeguards, engagement steps, monitoring measures, or review processes are needed to reduce equity and civil rights risks?

sections.title: Privacy, Security, Legal, Procurement, and Vendor Review

fields.label: Privacy review needed
fields.help_text: Explain whether protected, identifiable, confidential, or sensitive information is involved.

fields.label: Security review needed
fields.help_text: Explain hosting, access controls, authentication, logging, vendor access, cloud environment, or data transmission issues.

fields.label: Legal or policy review needed
fields.help_text: Note any statutory, regulatory, grant, data use, records, civil rights, procurement, or agency policy issues.

fields.label: Vendor involvement
fields.help_text: Name any vendor or external partner. Describe whether data would be shared, stored, processed, or used to train or improve vendor models.

fields.label: Transparency needs
fields.help_text: Would staff, partners, governance bodies, or the public need to be told that AI is being used? Describe what should be disclosed.

sections.title: Initial Risk Screening
sections.description: Use yes/no/unsure responses and notes to identify the minimum level of governance review needed.
risk_questions: Does the use case involve identifiable, protected, confidential, or sensitive information?
Could outputs influence services, eligibility, outreach, enforcement, resource allocation, public messaging, or public health action?
Could the use case affect communities differently or create disparate impact?
Will a vendor, external model, or cloud service process agency data?
Could incorrect output create harm, delay response, misinform the public, or undermine trust?
Does the workflow require formal human review, override, or documentation?
Is the use case already in use without documented governance review?
Does the proposed tool create new records, logs, prompts, or outputs that must be retained?

triage_levels.label: Low-risk internal productivity use
triage_levels.typical_characteristics: No sensitive data; no public-facing output; no consequential decision; human fully controls final work product.
triage_levels.minimum_review_pathway: Program supervisor or designated AI lead confirms acceptable use, data limits, and documentation.
triage_levels.possible_decision: Proceed with guardrails / revise / route to Level 2 if uncertainty remains.

triage_levels.label: Moderate operational use
triage_levels.typical_characteristics: May support workflow efficiency, summarization, analytics, or internal decisions; limited or controlled sensitive data; requires human review.
triage_levels.minimum_review_pathway: AI governance intake review plus privacy/security review if data or systems are involved.
triage_levels.possible_decision: Proceed to planning / revise / require additional review / pause.

triage_levels.label: High-impact or consequential use
triage_levels.typical_characteristics: May affect services, surveillance, resource allocation, public messaging, partner obligations, community outcomes, or public trust.
triage_levels.minimum_review_pathway: Formal AI governance review with privacy, security, legal, equity, program, technical, and operational reviewers.
triage_levels.possible_decision: Approve for limited pilot / require redesign / defer / reject.

triage_levels.label: Prohibited or not ready
triage_levels.typical_characteristics: Unclear public health purpose; unacceptable privacy/security/equity risk; no human accountability; vendor/data terms are not acceptable; or benefits do not justify risk.
triage_levels.minimum_review_pathway: Governance body documents reasons and conditions that would need to change before reconsideration.
triage_levels.possible_decision: Do not proceed / redesign and resubmit / escalate to leadership.

fields.label: Rationale
fields.help_text: Explain why this level was selected.

fields.label: Required evidence before next step
fields.help_text: List the documents, policies, testing results, data descriptions, vendor terms, or engagement evidence needed before approval.

fields.label: Conditions of approval
fields.help_text: List required safeguards, limits, training, monitoring, documentation, human review steps, or pilot constraints.

fields.label: Next steps and owner
fields.help_text: List action items, owners, and due dates.

fields.label: Monitoring expectations
fields.help_text: Define what will be monitored after approval, such as performance, equity, errors, complaints, user adoption, incidents, and public health value.

fields.label: Re-review trigger
fields.help_text: Identify events that require re-review: new data source, vendor change, workflow change, expansion, incident, public concern, performance issue, or policy change.

completed_example.title: AI-drafted respiratory virus surveillance briefing
completed_example.summary: A local health department proposes using a generative AI tool to help draft plain-language summaries of weekly respiratory virus surveillance trends for internal leadership and partner briefings. The tool would summarize de-identified aggregate trends from an internal dashboard and prior approved messages. Staff would review and approve all outputs before use.

fields.Public health problem: Staff spend several hours each week translating surveillance trends into leadership updates. Drafts vary in clarity and reading level, and the communications team is often brought in late.
fields.Proposed AI-supported activity: Use an approved AI tool to draft a plain-language summary of aggregate respiratory virus trends. The AI output would not be released directly. Staff would review, revise, and approve the final message.
fields.Data sources: Aggregate dashboard trends, weekly case counts, hospital utilization summaries, test positivity, and prior approved public health messaging. No identifiable data would be entered.
fields.Human review: An epidemiologist verifies accuracy and uncertainty. A communications specialist reviews plain language, tone, and audience fit. A program manager approves the final briefing.
fields.Equity and accessibility: Staff check reading level, language access needs, and whether the summary could stigmatize any population or geographic area.
fields.Initial triage level: Level 2: Moderate operational use. The use supports internal drafting and partner communication, uses aggregate data, and requires human review. Governance should confirm approved tool use, prompt/data limits, review steps, records expectations, and monitoring.
fields.Governance decision: Proceed with conditions: use only approved aggregate data; prohibit identifiable data; document human review; retain final approved summaries; monitor errors and staff feedback for 60 days before expanding.
